audio_transcript: This is a pre-trip inspection of the Caterpillar 320D. We're going to start with the undercarriage. The track shoes look to be in good condition, no excessive wear. Rollers appear to be properly lubricated. The idlers are aligned correctly. Sprockets show minimal wear. Moving on to the hydraulic system. Cylinders are not leaking. Hoses and fittings are secure. The bucket is in good shape. The cutting edge has some wear, but the teeth are still sharp. Side cutters are intact. Engine compartment check. Oil level is good. Coolant level is within the normal range. Belts are tight and show no signs of cracking. Filters appear clean. Boom and stick are structurally sound. No visible cracks or damage to the frame. Overall, this machine is in good condition.
